status: CANONICAL. This is the implementation contract. No code modification may exceed it without an explicit, recorded architectural approval.

epistemic_categories.note: *** EVERY statement in this contract carries exactly one of these. They are NEVER merged. ***
epistemic_categories.DERIVED_FACT: measured from the repository or the live tree at fcffa73. Reproducible.
epistemic_categories.ARCHITECTURAL_DECISION: a Cycle 1-5 finding, accepted as authoritative input to Cycle 6.
epistemic_categories.IMPLEMENTATION_DECISION: a Cycle-6 choice about HOW to build. Reversible; reasons stated.
epistemic_categories.HUMAN_APPROVAL: requires a named person to say yes in writing. NOT inferrable from code.

frozen_inputs.note: Cycles 1-5 are AUTHORITATIVE. Cycle 6 designed no architecture and discovered no defects. Where Cycle 6 contradicts an earlier cycle it is because the LIVE TREE was measured and the earlier cycle had not measured it — recorded in ../CYCLE6_CORRECTIONS.md.

citation_integrity.claim: *** All 16 load-bearing Cycle-4 file:line citations were RE-RESOLVED at fcffa73 and every one is CORRECT. ***
citation_integrity.why_it_matters: INV-20 established that 10 of 10 CLAUDE.md citations are STALE. Cycle 4's are NOT. The contract may therefore inherit them. A future cycle need not re-check.

live_state_at_contract_freeze.note: *** Cycle 4 named five read-only checks as merge gates and ran NONE of them. Cycle 6 ran all five. Three of them COLLAPSE a risk Cycle 4 rated as blocking. ***
live_state_at_contract_freeze.method: sqlite snapshot of the live ledger read from a scratch copy (live tree never opened for write); accounts.json parsed read-only; filesystem counted with ls/du.

consequences_that_change_the_program.collapses: *** THE S02 <-> S10 SEMANTIC CONFLICT — which Cycle 4 called 'the single most dangerous scope expansion in the sequence' and which forced S02 to carry a mandatory loud-log mitigation. ***

The hazard is NOT REACHABLE on this deployment today. RC-3 remains a REAL DEFECT (the load boundary has no validation, and the operator is a documented hand-editor — AR-09), but S02 is DECOUPLED FROM PD-3 on the current tree.

consequences_that_change_the_program.confirms: RC-10 / C3-F4. The leak is real, unbounded, and has been accumulating.
consequences_that_change_the_program.verdict: S09's one-time clean has a KNOWN, BOUNDED size. It is a DATA migration, and it is safe (EXP-C4-4 proved the self-heal).

*** The publish lifecycle HAS been exercised 73 times. *** RC-1 and RC-2 are not hypothetical paths — they sit on a road this system has driven. The current ledger shows 0 published ONLY because it was WIPED (see LS-7).
consequences_that_change_the_program.self_correction: C6-SC-1 — an earlier Cycle-6 draft concluded from the 0-published ledger that 'nothing has ever published on this tree'. THAT WAS FALSE and the archive refutes it.

consequences_that_change_the_program.measurement: *** 11 ledger snapshot/backup artifacts in 00_control/, spanning 2026-06-20 -> 2026-07-10 *** — including a genuine `ledger.snapshot.20260705T103618Z.1d5a6ffe.json` (the Ledger.snapshot naming convention) and `ledger.json.prewipe-20260629-000912`.
consequences_that_change_the_program.verdict: *** THIS IS THE SHARPEST FINDING OF CYCLE 6. *** The operator is a HEAVY, HABITUAL user of the snapshot / wipe / restore machinery — roughly monthly — with a launchd daemon installed. `ledger_wipe.py:246` advertises `restore_snapshot` as THE rollback path. *** S01's race (RC-4 / C4-F2a: SILENT MEDIA DELETION) is therefore not an exotic break-glass hazard. It is ON THE PROCEDURE THIS OPERATOR ACTUALLY RUNS. *** Cycle 4 rated it CRITICAL from the mechanism alone; the live tree confirms its reachability independently.
consequences_that_change_the_program.consequence: S01 is not merely FIRST by severity. It is first by DEMONSTRATED OPERATOR BEHAVIOUR.

GLOBAL_BOUNDARIES.note: *** These bind EVERY slice, including any slice a future cycle adds. They are the part of the contract Cycle 4 COULD NOT HAVE WRITTEN, because they derive from Cycle 5's dependency model — which did not exist when the slices were designed. ***

GB-1_no_lazy_import_hoist.boundary: *** NO SLICE MAY HOIST AN IN-FUNCTION (lazy) `fanops` IMPORT TO MODULE LEVEL. NO SLICE MAY ADD A MODULE-LEVEL `fanops` IMPORT THAT POINTS TO AN EQUAL-OR-HIGHER LAYER LEVEL. ***
GB-1_no_lazy_import_hoist.why: Cycle 5 (AST, whole-tree): 107 of 323 lazy edges point to an equal-or-higher level; *** 56 are STRICTLY UPWARD — true layering inversions. *** The SCC-condensed compile-time graph is an 11-level DAG ONLY BECAUSE those imports are deferred to call time. Module init order is LOAD-BEARING and *** NOTHING ENFORCES IT: no test, no lint rule, no layer declaration. ***
GB-1_no_lazy_import_hoist.the_trap: Hoisting one of the 56 is a change that *** LOOKS LIKE A CLEANUP AND THAT A REVIEWER WOULD WAVE THROUGH. *** It breaks the process at start.
GB-1_no_lazy_import_hoist.sharpest_instance: `config` — level 0, fan-in 82, THE most-depended-on module in the system — lazily reaches UP to `accounts` (L2) and `meta_graph` (L3). *** S02 TOUCHES `accounts`. ***
GB-1_no_lazy_import_hoist.enforceable_by_cycle_7: AST diff: for every added module-level `from fanops.X import` / `import fanops.X`, assert level(X) < level(current_module) using kb/dependencies.json -> layering.levels. Reject otherwise.

GB-2_no_dead_code_deletion_without_an_as.boundary: *** NO SLICE MAY DELETE A SYMBOL ON THE STRENGTH OF 'ZERO CALLERS'. *** 'Zero callers' is a LEAD, never a verdict.
GB-2_no_dead_code_deletion_without_an_as.why: `post/providers.py` dispatches SIX backend factories via lazy in-function import LAMBDAS from a dict. *** A name-based call graph flags ALL SIX as 'zero callers'. ALL SIX ARE LIVE. *** Same trap: `post.compress.persist_post_shrink` (lazy-imported from studio/actions.py).
slices_at_risk: S02 — touches post/__init__.py, which resolves through PROVIDERS
GB-2_no_dead_code_deletion_without_an_as.required_evidence: any deletion must ship an AST writer/caller census over the WHOLE tree (not a grep), per the Cycle-2 method note.

GB-3_no_extra_forbid_on_a_ledger_model.boundary: *** NO SLICE MAY SET `extra="forbid"` ON ANY LEDGER MODEL. ***
GB-3_no_extra_forbid_on_a_ledger_model.why: Forward-compat holds by pydantic's DEFAULT (`extra='ignore'`), NOT by declaration. Setting `extra='forbid'` — a change that LOOKS LIKE TIGHTENING — turns a forward-rolled ledger into a hard `ControlFileError` and bricks every reader.
slices_at_risk: S02 (its REJECTED Option C was exactly this)
S11 (it is ABOUT the validator boundary)

GB-4_no_new_door_to_the_R1_poison_pill.boundary: *** NO SLICE MAY CREATE A NEW WRITE PATH TO `PostState.published` OR `PostState.analyzed` WITHOUT AN EXPLICIT NON-EMPTY `public_url` GUARD AT THE CALL SITE. ***
GB-4_no_new_door_to_the_R1_poison_pill.why: The R1 invariant is enforced at CONSTRUCTION only. `model_copy(update=)` and `setattr` BOTH bypass every validator. The property survives via FOUR INDEPENDENT MANUAL CALL-SITE GUARDS. *** A FIFTH DOOR ADDED WITHOUT ONE SAVES CLEANLY AND THEN BRICKS THE NEXT `Ledger.load` — taking down the daemon and every Studio page at once. ***
slices_at_risk: S04 (model_copy on Post in the ladder)
GB-4_no_new_door_to_the_R1_poison_pill.pinned_by: S11 — which exists precisely to make this boundary MECHANICAL rather than a promise.

GB-5_no_moment_setattr_to_model_copy_con.boundary: *** NO SLICE MAY CONVERT A `setattr` ON A `Moment` TO `model_copy` — not even 'for consistency with the other 57 sites'. ***
GB-5_no_moment_setattr_to_model_copy_con.why: `Moment` is the ONLY model with `validate_assignment=True`. `model_copy` bypasses it ANYWAY. `actions_casting.cast_add/cast_remove` are correct ONLY because of that setattr. Converting them would silently stop canonicalizing affinities — and a non-canonical '@Foo' would make that moment post NOWHERE, silently.

GB-6_ast_ratchet_budgets.evidence_class: DERIVED_FACT (read from the test files at fcffa73)
GB-6_ast_ratchet_budgets.note: *** These are MECHANICALLY ENFORCED IN CI TODAY. Cycle 4 named the ratchets but never derived the PER-SLICE BUDGET, and never noticed that ONE OF THEM IS A SHARED, EXACT-EQUALITY BUDGET ACROSS THREE SLICES. ***

swallow_ratchet.mechanism: a PER-FILE baseline dict (49 files). It fails on (a) a file NOT in the baseline gaining ANY silent broad `except`, or (b) a baselined file EXCEEDING its count.
swallow_ratchet.rule: Every new `except` a slice adds MUST log or fail-open. This is the house norm and it is already enforced. NO slice may raise a baseline.

print_ratchet.mechanism_A: `_INTERNAL_MODULES` — nine modules that must contain ZERO `print()`. Assertion: `offenders == []`.

zero_print_modules_owned_by_a_slice.src/fanops/ledger.py: *** S01 MAY ADD NO print() TO ledger.py. ***
zero_print_modules_owned_by_a_slice.src/fanops/pipeline.py: *** S07 MAY ADD NO print() TO pipeline.py. ***

print_ratchet.mechanism_B: `_CLI_PRINT_COUNT = 168`, asserted with *** EXACT EQUALITY *** (`assert len(_print_call_nodes(cli.py)) == 168`). Not a ceiling — an EQUALITY.
print_ratchet.CYCLE_7_CORRECTION: *** THIS NUMBER WAS 147 AND IT WAS WRONG. *** The contract froze it at fcffa73 as a DERIVED_FACT 'read from the test files'. ONE COMMIT LATER (#634) the test file said 158, and the contract still said 147. A boundary pinned to a wrong constant is not a boundary — a slice obeying GB-6 as written would have been told the budget was 147 when CI enforces 158. It is now DERIVED_FROM_CONTRACT: regenerated into derived/ratchets.json and enforced by rule IMPL-007, which FAILS CI when this copy disagrees with the test that enforces it. *** MIGRATION TARGET: delete this copy entirely and point at derived/ratchets.json. A copied number is a number that rots, and this one rotted in a single commit. ***

THE_CROSS_SLICE_HAZARD.statement: *** S08, S09 AND S10 ALL TOUCH cli.py. If two of them land carrying a bumped `_CLI_PRINT_COUNT`, the second one's rebase produces a WRONG constant and CI goes RED for a reason unrelated to its change. ***
THE_CROSS_SLICE_HAZARD.THE_RULE_THAT_DISSOLVES_IT: *** NO SLICE MAY CHANGE cli.py's print() COUNT. *** New operator output routes through the existing logger, or reuses an existing print. This is free — none of S08/S09/S10 NEEDS a new print — and it removes the coupling entirely rather than serializing around it.
THE_CROSS_SLICE_HAZARD.escape_hatch: If a slice PROVES it must add one: it becomes a SERIALIZING constraint. At most ONE open PR may modify `_CLI_PRINT_COUNT`, and it must carry a rationale comment (the existing file convention). Cycle 7 enforces: reject a second concurrent PR touching that constant.

rules: *** NEVER run the test suite locally. *** pytest runs ONLY in GitHub CI on a PR. Parallel workers running the suite crash the machine. `./scripts/check.sh` is lint-only.
*** NEVER mass-reformat. *** No black, no `ruff format`. The compact one-liner house style (E701/E702/E401/E501 ignored) is deliberate.
A non-MOL PR title MUST carry `(Unit: <slug>)`; the lowercased slug must match the verifier record filename.
ONE orchestrator landing session at a time. `git fetch` + `gh pr view` the target before merge.
The 60s pytest timeout is a DEADLOCK GUARDRAIL. A hanging test is the bug — do not raise the timeout to make it pass.
Do NOT run live CLI verbs (publish, reconcile, pull, track, up, verify-live, cutover) speculatively.

slice_classes.REMEDIATION: changes runtime behaviour to close an approved root cause.
slice_classes.GUARD: *** changes NO runtime behaviour. *** Adds mechanical enforcement so a closed (or unreachable) hazard cannot silently re-open. A GUARD slice is how an UNREACHABLE root cause is discharged WITHOUT over-engineering a fix for a bug nobody can hit.

slices.title: restore_snapshot writes the snapshot IN PLACE, under the ledger's own lock
slices.architectural_objective: INT-5 — replacing the ledger's contents is mutually exclusive with every active writer, and never orphans an inode a writer holds open.
slices.blast_radius: THE LEDGER WRITE PATH. The highest-consequence code in the persistence layer.
slices.reachability_on_the_live_tree: *** CONFIRMED (LS-7). 11 snapshot/backup artifacts; the operator runs this procedure ~monthly with a daemon installed. ***

slices.title: Canonical backend normalization at the READ boundary
slices.architectural_objective: INT-3 — a live account with an unsupported provider value must fail BEFORE a post is claimed.
slices.blast_radius: WHICH CHANNELS PUBLISH LIVE. A real, external side effect.
slices.reachability_on_the_live_tree: *** THE DEFECT IS REACHABLE (the load boundary has no validation, and hand-editing is the DOCUMENTED operator channel — AR-09). THE S02<->S10 HAZARD IS NOT: 0 malformed values exist today (LS-3). ***

slices.title: The publish CLAIM refuses a post it will not POST
slices.architectural_objective: INT-1 — a post is claimed to `submitting` ONLY when the very next action is a network POST for that post.
slices.blast_radius: THE PUBLISH PATH. Strictly REMOVES a state transition; adds none.

slices.title: The reconcile terminal ladder is a pure function of (state, age)
slices.architectural_objective: INT-2 — every post in {submitting, submitted, needs_reconcile} reaches a terminal or retryable state in bounded time, REGARDLESS of poll outcome, token provenance, or error_reason.
slices.blast_radius: THE TERMINAL SEMANTICS OF THE PUBLISH LIFECYCLE.
slices.migration_size_on_the_live_tree: *** 0 ROWS (LS-1). Cycle 4 assumed 347. The reconcilable set is EMPTY. ***
approvals_required: PD-2 (shape decided; recommendation: the same age ladder)

slices.title: Status-client parity: an unknown id is `unknown`, not an error
slices.architectural_objective: INT-2 (defence in depth). Absorb a REMOTE-API shape difference at the FanOps boundary.
slices.why: *** Alone it fixes the fake-token Zernio case and leaves BOTH the real-token case AND the 500-forever case unterminated — the brief's explicitly-forbidden shallow fix. ***
slices.blast_radius: one return branch of one client.
slices.self_merge_condition: ONLY if S04 is already merged.

slices.title: A recovery action must not leave a post carrying a stale status string
slices.architectural_objective: INT-7 — no recovery action leaves a post carrying a status string describing a state it is no longer in.
co_required_with: S04 (which owns the reconcile-side half, reconcile.py:767)
slices.blast_radius: ONE FIELD on ONE revert path. One line.

slices.title: Reconcile and publish are gated on the same predicate
slices.architectural_objective: INT-4 — the producer of a state and its SOLE consumer are gated on the SAME predicate.

slices.title: Daemon health: `alive` must not mean `succeeding`
slices.architectural_objective: INT-6 — a verdict named `alive` must not imply successful work unless a successful-pass timestamp is independently reported.
slices.blast_radius: AN OBSERVABILITY SURFACE. Adds a signal; moves none.

slices.title: The shrink temp dir gets an owner
slices.architectural_objective: INT-8 — no persisted path may point into storage whose lifetime is shorter than the ledger record.

slices.title: Irreversible retirement policy
slices.status: *** BLOCKED ON PD-3. DO NOT EXECUTE. ***
prerequisites: PD-3 answered in writing by the operator
slices.accrued_cost_on_the_live_tree: *** ZERO moments retired (LS-2). The actuator has never fired. ***

slices.title: Pin the mutation/validator boundary — RC-9 cannot silently become reachable
slices.architectural_objective: INT-9 (PINNED, not FIXED) — a validator that raises at construction must raise on every mutation API. *** RC-9 has ZERO current reachability. This slice does NOT fix it. It makes its unreachability MECHANICAL. ***
slices.blast_radius: *** NONE. Test-only. Zero runtime behaviour change. ***
slices.why_this_slice_exists: *** Cycle 4 has TEN root causes and TEN slices — but the mapping is NOT a bijection. RC-4+RC-5 collapse into S01, and RC-9 MAPS TO NOTHING. It was deferred ('zero reachability, sequenced last') and never sliced. The Cycle-6 completion standard FORBIDS an orphaned root cause. ***
slices.why_a_GUARD_and_not_a_FIX: *** Fixing RC-9 means adding validate_assignment / a validated-mutation API across 8 models and ~57 model_copy sites, to close a bug WITH ZERO CURRENT REACHABILITY — while RC-4 silently deletes media. That is textbook over-engineering, and Cycle 4 was RIGHT to defer it. But 'we checked, it's unreachable' ROTS. A CI policy test does not. ***

slices.title: Pin the layering — a lazy import cannot be hoisted without CI going red
slices.traces_to: AR-04 (Cycle 5 architectural RISK, *** NOT an approved Cycle-4 root cause ***)
slices.status: *** PROPOSED — NOT APPROVED. REQUIRES AN EXPLICIT ARCHITECTURAL APPROVAL BEFORE EXECUTION. ***
slices.why_it_needs_approval: *** The Cycle-6 brief requires that every code modification be traceable to an APPROVED ROOT CAUSE. AR-04 is a Cycle-5 RISK. It is not in the RC set. Slipping S12 into the program without saying so would be exactly the hidden scope expansion this contract exists to prevent. It is surfaced as a DECISION, not smuggled in as a fix. ***
prerequisites: HUMAN_APPROVAL: promote AR-04 to an approved root cause, OR reject S12

implementation_dag.proof: Edges: S03->S04, S04->S05, S03->S06, S02->S07. Roots: S01, S02, S03, S08, S09, S11, (S12). No back edge exists. Tarjan over this edge set returns 12 singleton SCCs.

ordering_edges.why: S03 stops the strand being CREATED; S04 drains those ALREADY stranded. S04-first would escalate a churn of posts publish immediately re-strands. Noisy, not dangerous — but avoidable. *** Stop the bleeding, then drain. ***
SUPERSEDES. Once S04 hoists the terminal out of the poll-outcome branch, S05 is no longer load-bearing. S05 ALONE is a forbidden shallow fix.
S06 keeps preserving submission_id ('keep the lineage'). That is SAFE ONLY BECAUSE S03 makes the claim refuse a real-sid post. S06 without S03 is not harmful — merely incomplete.
S02 SHRINKS S07's reachability. Landing S07 first is not wrong, but its fixtures would encode a malformed-backend world S02 then eliminates.

co_requirements_NOT_ordering_edges.note: *** A co-requirement is NOT a cycle. Both slices are required for the INVARIANT to hold; NEITHER must precede the other. Modelling these as ordering edges would MANUFACTURE a cycle that does not exist — exactly the C5-SC-2 error, applied to the implementation graph. ***

pairs.why: If _reconcile_safe is gated OFF, S04's ladder NEVER RUNS. S04 is correct but UNREACHABLE until S07 lands. Neither breaks the other.
S04 owns reconcile.py:767 (the suppression key). S06 owns actions.py:944-951 (the stale reason). No file conflict. INT-7 holds only when BOTH land.

implementation_dag.reframe_stream: *** VERIFIED DISJOINT by STATE OWNERSHIP, not by filename (Cycle 4). framing.py and keyframes.py have ZERO ledger references; clip.py's only three `Render` tokens are docstrings. LS-4 re-confirms IF-1 against the LIVE TREE: 0 renders, 0/347 posts with a render_id. Cycle-6 implementation may proceed alongside reframe work. ***

recommended_landing_order.note: *** This CHANGES Cycle 4's phasing, and the change is driven by MEASUREMENT, not taste. ***

order.why: *** The only CRITICAL, and LS-7 proves it sits on the procedure the operator actually runs. Nothing should land ahead of it. ***

*** FREE and it PROTECTS every slice that follows. S03/S04/S06 all mutate Post; S11 makes GB-4 mechanical BEFORE they land, not after. Test-only, zero blast radius, self-merges on green. Landing it second is the cheapest risk reduction in the program. ***

*** THE WINDOW IS NOW. LS-1: the reconcilable set is EMPTY (0 rows). S04's migration is a NO-OP TODAY. The moment the operator approves any of the 347 awaiting_approval posts and it publishes, S04 acquires a real blast radius. *** Close the submission lifecycle BEFORE it is next exercised. Sequential within.
order.operational_gate: S04 still ships report-only mode FIRST — as a PERMANENT gate, not because of today's count.

order.why: S02 before S07. LS-3 decouples S02 from PD-3 on the current tree — re-verify at merge.
Independent; buildable any time. Placed here only by severity.

recommended_landing_order.supersedes: Cycle 4's Phase 2 (S02+S07) ran BEFORE Phase 3 (the submission lifecycle). Cycle 6 REVERSES that, because (a) LS-1 makes the lifecycle chain a zero-migration change TODAY and that window closes on first publish, and (b) LS-3 removes the urgency that put S02 early (it was early partly to de-risk the malformed-backend blast radius — which does not exist).

PD-1.question: Does the operator need an explicit 'republish (mint a new submission)' action?

PD-2.question: What is the correct terminal for a real-token post the backend never resolves?
PD-2.recommendation: The SAME age ladder. `GAVE UP:` is a LABEL written to error_reason — it changes NO state, so a given-up post is NOT re-queueable and CANNOT double-post.

PD-3.question: Is `adjust.retire`'s irreversible MomentState.retired at n=3 analyzed posts intentional policy, or an oversight?
PD-3.recommendation: *** NONE OFFERED. *** Not recoverable from code. The guards that exist (bottom-20% AND lift<20.0 AND not-a-winner AND not-lift_degraded) are real and considered — which is EVIDENCE FOR INTENT.
PD-3.new_evidence_from_cycle_6: *** It has cost NOTHING so far: 0 moments retired (LS-2). And S02 cannot unfreeze it: 0 malformed backends (LS-3). The decision is no less REQUIRED — but it is no longer URGENT, and Cycle 4's mandatory S02 mitigation is DISARMED on the current tree. ***

PD-4.question: On a malformed backend at load: normalize-and-skip, or raise?
PD-4.recommendation: Normalize case/whitespace; SKIP-and-flag an unknown name via the EXISTING `skipped_rows` channel. *** This is not new policy — it moves set_backend's existing rule to the door that lacks it. ***

PD-5_NEW.question: *** Is S12 (the AR-04 layering guard) in scope? *** AR-04 is a Cycle-5 architectural RISK, not an approved Cycle-4 root cause. Building it without approval would be a hidden scope expansion.
PD-5_NEW.recommendation: *** APPROVE. *** GB-1 is already a MANDATORY boundary on all 11 other slices; S12 merely makes it mechanical instead of a promise. It is test-only, zero blast radius, and it protects every future change. But it is the OPERATOR's call, and it is surfaced rather than smuggled.

operational_constraint.cost_to_cycle_6: Cycle 6 was executed SINGLE-THREADED, like Cycles 1-5. No independent verifier could be spawned to REFUTE this contract's boundaries.
operational_constraint.mitigation_applied: Cycle 6 grounded every collapsible risk in a DIRECT MEASUREMENT of the live tree rather than in an argument. A measurement does not need a second opinion in the way an inference does.
operational_constraint.disengage: `orchestrate.py stop` — AN OPERATOR ACTION, not a code change.
